item.title: I’m de-influencing you from buying the RingConn 3 (even though it’s pretty)
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: The RingConn 3 actually looks like real jewelry, not a wearable -- but its fitness tracking and headache detection features are disappointing.

item.title: Reflection inks $1B compute deal with Nebius
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Reflection AI has signed a $1 billion deal to access Nebius's compute. Reflection was founded in 2024 and is developing open source AI technology.

item.title: The real AI race may no longer be at the frontier
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue says enterprises increasingly want open models, due to cost, accessibility, and ownership. Do frontier models still matter if most production AI ends up running on open models?

item.title: Telegram’s shortlink domain is back online after day-long suspension
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Telegram CEO Pavel Durov confirmed an outage in a tweet, saying that shortlinks to the messaging app had "stopped working."

item.title: Spotify expands its AI push with a ChatGPT-like music assistant
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Spotify is rolling out a new AI-powered conversational feature that lets Premium subscribers chat with the app to discover music, podcasts, audiobooks, and more.

item.title: Superhuman’s new auto-draft feature almost makes me like AI replies
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Superhuman’s latest AI email drafting feature is its most convincing yet, generating replies that often required little to no editing in our testing.

item.title: David Beckham’s health drink startup IM8 takes $1B from General Catalyst’s unusual CVF fund
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: General Catalyst’s Customer Value Fund doesn't make equity investments. It's providing $1 billion for IM8, known for its longevity vitamin drink.

item.title: Pinwheel launches a retro-inspired landline phone for kids
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Kid-friendly tech company Pinwheel announced the launch of a new landline phone designed to let children stay connected without the distractions of a smartphone.

item.title: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.

item.title: Uber’s product chief on hotels, robotaxis, and why the company doesn’t want to be ‘everything for everyone’
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Uber Chief Product Officer Sachin Kansal walks TechCrunch through the company's financial services ambitions, its increasingly complicated relationship with Waymo, its new AV Labs data operation, and how AI is starting to show up in ways riders and drivers will actually notice.

item.title: Video-generation startup PixVerse raises $439M, valuation soars past $2B
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: With the cash, the company aims to expand its world model offering and reach customers across geographies.

item.title: X just tweaked its algorithm to make it more friendly, less battleground
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The social media site says it will amplify posts made by users' mutual followers' to give the feed more of a communal feel.

item.title: Hermes agent maker Nous Research in talks for new funding at $1.5B valuation
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta, Marina Temkin
item.description: The company is raising at least $75 million, led by Robot Ventures, with significant participation from USV and other prominent investors.

item.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI labs that sell proprietary models are somehow acting like Trojan horses.

item.title: Apple says former employee exploited ‘rare’ bug to download confidential files after leaving for OpenAI
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Apple would not comment on the "security breach," which allegedly allowed a former employee to download sensitive files from Apple's network long after he departed the company for rival OpenAI.

item.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple hardware to interviews. Here are the complaint’s most eye-catching claims.

item.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."

item.title: Investors send General Fusion soaring in debut as first publicly traded fusion company
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: General Fusion started trading on the Nasdaq following a reverse merger that saw high redemptions.

item.title: 12 states sue to block Paramount’s $110B Warner Bros. deal
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: The states allege that the deal would harm movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and audiences.

item.title: Should AI help you get away with killing your spouse?
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: What does a world of total user-aligned AI actually look like?
